channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Marine Corps Tests Helicopter-Deployed Drone Command System
item.description: Marines successfully tested controlling FPV attack drones from a UH-1Y Venom helicopter in California, extending the reach of low-cost drone systems in a new integration of aviation and unmanned capabilities.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Identified as Fentanyl Smuggling Route to US, DEA Official Says
item.description: A Drug Enforcement Administration official confirmed that Japan is part of the fentanyl trafficking route to the United States, marking the first US recognition of Japan's role in the synthetic opioid supply chain.

item.title: 🇶🇦 Qatar Sends Negotiation Team to Tehran to Push US-Iran Peace Deal
item.description: A Qatari delegation arrived in Tehran in coordination with Washington to help advance negotiations aimed at ending the Iran war, joining Pakistan as key mediators in the diplomatic process.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Gabbard stepped down citing her husband's cancer diagnosis, ending a tenure marked by tensions over the Iran war. Her cautious stance on US military action had put her at odds with Trump's decision to attack Iran.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Johor State Election Set for Three-Way Fight as Malaysian Opposition Declares Full Contest
item.description: Perikatan Nasional announced it will contest all 56 seats in Johor's upcoming state election, setting up a potential three-cornered battle with ruling coalitions PH and BN in Malaysia's most-watched state polls.

item.title: 🏛️ Warsh Takes Charge of Federal Reserve Facing Rising Inflation Threat
item.description: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Federal Reserve Chair at a pivotal moment, inheriting an economic backdrop that does not call for the interest rate cuts many had anticipated. The former Fed governor faces persistent inflation and market uncertainty.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Rubio Reports Slight Progress in Iran Talks, Rejects Hormuz Tolling System
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio signaled cautious progress in US-Iran peace talks mediated by Pakistan, but firmly rejected Irans proposal for a tolling system at the Strait of Hormuz. Key sticking points remain over enriched uranium and control of the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇧🇾 Lukashenko Vows Belarus Will Not Be Dragged Into Ukraine War
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko stated Belarus would not enter the Ukraine war, but affirmed joint defense with Russia against aggression. The statement comes amid Western concerns about Belarusian complicity in Moscows war effort.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Switzerland Expands Sanctions Against Russia and Belarus
item.description: Switzerland widened its sanctions lists against Russia and Belarus, aligning with the EUs latest measures over the war in Ukraine. The move strengthens European economic pressure on Moscow.

item.title: 🏛️ Kevin Warsh Sworn In as Federal Reserve Chair Amid Rising Inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair at a pivotal moment for the US economy, facing persistent inflation and market volatility. Analysts expect no rate cuts in 2026 as inflation risks linger.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO Allies Bewildered by Trumps Sudden Troop Deployment Reversals
item.description: NATO allies expressed confusion over President Trumps contradictory announcements on US troop movements in Europe, first ordering withdrawals then reversing course. Secretary Rubio is working to reassure allies during meetings in Sweden.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Tulsi Gabbard resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Gabbard announced she will step down on June 30 to help care for her husband, who was diagnosed with bone cancer. Trump named Aaron Lukas as acting DNI chief.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta to hold referendum on independence from Canada
item.description: Voters in the western province will decide next October whether to remain in Canada or pursue a binding independence referendum, amid growing separatist sentiment.

item.title: 🇦🇫 Taliban enacts law legitimizing child marriage, activists warn
item.description: A new Taliban law makes divorce impossible if husbands disagree, with up to 70% of girls potentially in early or forced marriages. Rights groups condemned the move.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey shuts down liberal Istanbul university in criminal probe
item.description: The Council of Higher Education closed Bilgi University after a criminal investigation, promising measures to ensure students suffer no harm.

item.title: 🇸🇳 Senegal prime minister defends LGBTQ crackdown, slams Western pressure
item.description: Senegal PM Ousmane Sonko denounced "homosexual tyranny" as human rights groups criticized legislation targeting same-sex relationships.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon military pushes back against US sanctions, affirms loyalty to state
item.description: Lebanons army and General Security responded to US sanctions by emphasizing their loyalty to state institutions, amid growing pressure on Lebanese armed forces over alleged Hezbollah ties.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israel admits killing three Palestinians in Gaza despite ceasefire
item.description: The Israeli military acknowledged killing three Palestinians in Gaza since the ceasefire was declared, bringing the total death toll to at least 883 Gazans killed since October.

item.title: 📉 US consumer sentiment hits record low amid Iran conflict anxiety
item.description: American consumer confidence fell to its lowest level on record as the ongoing conflict with Iran fuels economic uncertainty and inflation fears, according to a new survey.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US and Iran See Limited Progress in Geneva Talks
item.description: The US Secretary of State acknowledged slight progress in talks with Iran amid uncertainty over whether a diplomatic resolution can be reached. Iran has accused Washington of preparing fresh attacks while mediators work toward a framework to extend the pause in fighting.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Not Rushing to Extend China Trade Truce
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the Trump administration is not in a hurry to extend the tariff and critical minerals trade truce with China that expires in November. The comments suggest Washington is taking a measured approach to ongoing trade negotiations with Beijing.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Rubio Reports Progress in Iran Talks but Says Deal Not Yet Finalized
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday there has been "some progress" toward a deal with Iran but emphasized that more work is required, as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the conflict that has roiled the global economy.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵 China Cuts Off Japan From Rare Earths in Taiwan-Linked Dispute
item.description: China has blocked Japan's access to several heavy rare earths for at least four months, coinciding with tensions over Taiwan, in a move reminiscent of Beijing's 2010 export restrictions that highlights the strategic weaponization of critical mineral supply chains.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Left OPEC to Maximize Oil Revenues Before Hydrocarbon Age Ends
item.description: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC was three years in the making and reflects its view that the world is near the "autumn of the hydrocarbon age," meaning the country needs to maximize oil revenues while demand remains strong, a senior presidential adviser said.

item.title: 🇮🇱🚢 Gaza Flotilla Activists Allege Israeli Abuse Including Sexual Assault
item.description: Activists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a Gaza aid flotilla reported being subjected to abuse, with several hospitalized and at least 15 reporting sexual assaults, allegations that have drawn international condemnation.
